page: Vultr vcg-l40s-16c-180g-48vram is a GPU-accelerated cloud server designed for compute-heavy and graphics-intensive workloads. The instance is powered by an Intel x86_64 processor configuration providing 16 vCPUs across 16 physical cores with a single thread per core. The CPU allocation is shared, which represents a potential performance tradeoff for sustained, CPU-bound processing. This processing core is paired with 180.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 11.25 GB of RAM per core.

The primary accelerator is a single NVIDIA L40S GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, equipped with 48 GB of dedicated VRAM. This hardware configuration is bundled with 1200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, providing high-speed read and write capabilities for large datasets. Additionally, the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Given the combination of a high-capacity Ada Lovelace GPU, substantial system memory, and fast NVMe storage, this server is optimized for workloads such as machine learning model training, large language model inference, 3D rendering, and data science pipelines. The shared CPU architecture makes it a cost-efficient option for GPU-centric tasks where the primary computational bottleneck is offloaded to the accelerator rather than the host processor.
description: Vultr vcg-l40s-16c-180g-48vram is a GPU-accelerated cloud server featuring 16 Intel x86_64 vCPUs, 180.0 GB of system memory, and a bundled 1200 GB NVMe SSD. The system is equipped with a single NVIDIA L40S GPU from the Ada Lovelace architecture, providing 48 GB of dedicated VRAM. Operating with a shared CPU allocation and a single thread per core, the server offers a high memory-to-core ratio of 11.25 GB per core. This hardware profile is designed for GPU-intensive workloads, including machine learning training, deep learning inference, 3D rendering, and data science applications. The shared CPU configuration provides a cost-efficient entry point for projects where the primary computational demands are handled by the dedicated GPU accelerator rather than host processing resources.
og_description: Vultr vcg-l40s-16c-180g-48vram is a GPU-accelerated cloud server featuring 16 Intel vCPUs, 180 GB RAM, a 1200 GB NVMe SSD, and one NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB VRAM for AI and rendering workloads.

tagline: A GPU-accelerated virtual server featuring an NVIDIA L40S GPU, high memory capacity, and fast NVMe storage for demanding compute workloads.

16 Intel x86_64 vCPUs with shared allocation
180 GB system memory providing 11.25 GB per core
